The '''Kathol''', also known as the '''Precursors''', the '''Old Ones''',<ref name="Endgame">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Nears Its End|book=Endgame}}</ref> or '''Cthol''',<ref name="Gamemaster Handbook">''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Handbook]]''</ref> were an ancient race who lived on the planet [[Kathol (planet)|Kathol]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. They possessed organic technology and were a highly advanced civilization, proven when they created the [[Codex]]. Thousands of years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], several [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] attempted to conquer the Kathol. They were defeated, but the Kathol were almost wiped out. Most of the Kathol were able to store themselves in the [[Lifewell]], guarded by [[DarkStryder]]. Unfortunately, DarkStryder refused to release the Kathol after the danger had passed, and the Kathol were trapped in a form of limbo. Their life force became the mystical [[Ta-Ree]] energy.

Only after DarkStryder was destroyed by the crew of the ''[[FarStar]]'' were the Kathol freed. However, they were unable to reclaim corporeal form, and instead chose to dissipate themselves. A few hundred decided to live, but were constantly at odds with each other.

As mentioned, the Kathol possessed advanced technology and were capable of growing advanced biological machines. They crafted several servant species to serve them which included the:
*[[Charr Ontee]]
*[[DarkStryder]]
*[[Maoi]]

The Darkstryder in turn crafted for itself a few servants which included the:
*[[Krakai]]
*[[Segmi]]
*[[Yapi]]
*[[Yimi]]
*[[Zizimaak]]

The name "Cthol" was considered a mispronunciation of "Kathol", but still continued to appear in common usage up to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] due to being written so in erroneous documents.<ref name="TKR">''[[The Kathol Rift]]''</ref>

{{Species-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The rough outline of the Kathol species is familiar to many [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HP_Lovecraft HP Lovecraft] and [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D%26D Dungeons & Dragons] fans. Not only were the Kathol created for a type of source literature that is typically used for Dungeons & Dragons style roleplaying adventures, the Kathol themselves bear more than a passing resemblance to the story of [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cthulhu Cthulhu], a fictional creation of Lovecraft. Indeed, the Kathol's name is even referenced in a manner similar to Cthulhu, written simply as Cthol. The Kathol species descend from the [[Old Ones]], while Cthulhu is a member of the [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Old_One Great Old Ones]. Both the Kathol and Cthulhu are imprisoned against their will in a remote region, one in space, and one on Earth. In addition, both the Kathol and Cthulhu have a form of cult-worship following; the Aing-Tii for the Kathol, and the Cult of Cthulhu for Cthulhu itself.
